description: A dev build serving stale cached output so source edits appear to have no effect, sending the developer down a false debugging path.
body: Failure mode: the dev server or bundler serves a cached/previous build because the watcher missed the change or a cache was not invalidated, so edits seem ignored and the developer debugs code that is not actually running. Detection signals: changes only appear after a manual restart or cache clear; the watcher silently missed the file; hashes/timestamps show stale assets. Do not keep editing assuming the new code runs. Safer fallback: confirm the watcher covers the edited path, clear the build cache, restart the dev process, and verify the change is live before debugging further. Risk boundary: when reload reliability is uncertain, force a clean rebuild rather than trusting cached output.
